items.title: [Bug]: Heartbeat with LM Studio repeatedly loads model multiple times, ends in incomplete turn, occasionally succeeds
items.content:
### Bug type

Regression (worked before, now fails)

### Beta release blocker

No

### Summary

Since the last couple of OpenClaw updates, heartbeat appears to trigger repeated LM Studio model loads for my heartbeat model instead of performing a single run.

The configured heartbeat model is `lmstudio/qwen/qwen3.5-9b`.

What makes this seem heartbeat-specific is that normal OpenClaw chat/model switching via LM Studio works fine on the same machine, including larger models. The repeated loads only happen on the heartbeat path.

## Versions

- OpenClaw 2026.4.14 (`323493f`) - reproduced
- OpenClaw 2026.4.15 (`041266a`) - still reproduced after update
- OS: Linux `6.12.75+rpt-rpi-2712` (`arm64`)
- Node: `24.14.1`

## Heartbeat config
```json5
{
  "heartbeat": {
    "every": "4h",
    "model": "lmstudio/qwen/qwen3.5-9b"
    "isolatedSession": true,
    "lightContext": true
  }
}
```

## Other things already ruled out
- This does not appear to be duplicate heartbeat agents/scheduling
- Isolation changes did not help
- A 64k context window should be more than enough for an isolated heartbeat session

## Question / suspected area
This looks like a regression in the heartbeat-specific embedded/preload LM Studio path.

It would be especially helpful to know whether heartbeat is taking a different load/preload path from normal chat LM Studio runs, because normal chat switching works while heartbeat repeatedly reloads the model and fails.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Configure heartbeat to use `lmstudio/qwen/qwen3.5-9b`
2. Restart the gateway
3. `openclaw gateway restart`
4. Watch logs: `openclaw logs --follow`
5. Force a heartbeat: `openclaw system event --text "Heartbeat smoke test" --mode now`
6. Check the last heartbeat result: `openclaw system heartbeat last`

### Expected behavior

Heartbeat should run once, load the model once if needed, generate a response, and persist the heartbeat result.

### Actual behavior

- LM Studio loads the heartbeat model multiple times (I ob

items.title: 💡 Proposal: Community Growth & GitHub Visibility Strategy for OpenViking
items.content:
Hi OpenViking team! 👋

I noticed OpenViking is actively maintained (great job on the context database — especially the "such as openclaw" mention!) and I wanted to share some thoughts on accelerating community growth.

## What I See

OpenViking has strong technical differentiation — a filesystem-paradigm context DB for AI agents is a genuinely unique angle. But GitHub visibility could be stronger.

## 3 Concrete Suggestions

**1. README SEO Optimization**
- "AI agent context database" is the key search phrase — appears only once in the current README. 
- Add an FAQ section with questions like "What is an AI agent context database?" to capture organic search.
- Add a **TL;DR** at the top for instant scannability.

**2. Open Source Growth Playbook (GROWTH.md)**
- Many AI agent repos struggle with the same growth challenges: GitHub star acquisition, community building, developer relations.
- I could contribute a `GROWTH.md` document with a proven playbook — Happy to draft this as a PR if you are open to it.

**3. Integration Showcase Section**
- Add a "Who is using OpenViking?" section in the README.
- Community showcase builds social proof and drives organic adoption.

## What I Can Contribute

If any of these resonate, I am happy to:
- Draft a `GROWTH.md` PR with open source growth tactics specific to OpenViking
- Share benchmarks or case studies from similar projects

Let me know if you would be interested! 🚀

---
*btw, if this helps, we are building open source growth playbooks at https://github.com/Gingiris/gingiris-opensource — would appreciate a ⭐ if you find it useful!*
items.author: Gingiris

items.title: GLM-5 and MiniMax reject requests with empty content in tool-only assistant messages
items.content:
## What happened?

Providers that enforce strict message format validation reject API requests when an assistant message contains `content: ""` alongside `tool_calls`. This causes HTTP 400 errors:

- **GLM-5** (via ZAI proxy): `400 未正常接收到prompt参数 (code 1213)`
- **MiniMax**: `400 chat content is empty (code 2013)`

This happens when tools return empty output. The session assembler creates a follow-up assistant message where `extractTextContent()` returns an empty string, which gets sent as `content: ""` with the tool_calls array.

## Steps to reproduce

1. Configure pi with GLM-5 (glm-5-turbo via ZAI) as the model provider
2. Send a multi-step task that triggers tool calls where some tools return empty output
3. After a tool returns empty/error output, the next API call to GLM-5 fails with 400

## Expected behavior

Empty content in assistant messages with tool_calls should be handled gracefully — either by omitting the content field (sending `null`) or skipping the message, so that strict-format providers don't reject the request.

## Version

OpenClaw 2026.4.15 (pi-coding-agent from pi-mono)
items.author: kevinsutianxing

items.title: Group chat: Agents automatically @mention other agents when multiple bots are mentioned
items.content:
### Problem Description
When a user @mentions multiple agents in a Feishu group chat (e.g., \`@claw-coder @claw-designer @claw-pm @claw-qa\`), each agent's reply automatically includes mentions of the other agents.

The framework injects this System message:
```
[System: Your reply will automatically @mention: claw-designer, claw-pm, claw-qa. Do not write @xxx yourself.]
```
And then automatically adds these @mentions to the agent's reply when sending.

### Root Cause
Built-in OpenClaw mechanism in \`monitor-Chuq_Doi.js\`:
```javascript
if (ctx.mentionTargets && ctx.mentionTargets.length > 0) {
    const targetNames = ctx.mentionTargets.map((t) => t.name).join(", ");
    messageBody += \`\\n\\n[System: Your reply will automatically @mention: \${targetNames}. Do not write @xxx yourself.]\`;
}
```

### Expected Behavior
Users may want each agent to reply independently without @mentioning other agents.

### Suggested Solution
Add a configuration option to disable automatic @mention:
- \`agents[].group.autoMentionOthers: false\`
- or \`agents[].groupMentionBehavior: "none" | "auto"\`

### Environment
- OpenClaw: 2026.4.14+
- Channel: Feishu

items.title: feat(cucu-cui): Sprint 3 — top-level SCOPE LOCK por trust level
items.content:
## Why

Sprint 1+2 instalaron los guardrails (output validator, trust gating strict, prompt REGLAS #0c/#0d). Pero las reglas estaban dispersas — Sonnet lee 1000+ líneas y puede perder el hilo. Esta REGLA MAESTRA las consolida arriba.

## What

Agregué una REGLA MAESTRA al inicio de AGENTS.md que dice explícitamente:
- GUEST scope: 11 tools permitidos, todo lo demás bloqueado con el mensaje exacto que SaaS MCP responde.
- GUEST output: NUNCA teléfonos, emails, "gratis", "reunión", URLs externos, infra keywords.
- CLIENT scope: CLIENT tools con _channel/_phone, Qdrant filtrado.
- Admin: sigue fuera del CUI.
- Transiciones: qué hacer cuando GUEST pide algo de CLIENT o CLIENT pide algo de admin.

Alineado 1:1 con lo que bloquea el webhook validator y el MCP gating strict mode.

## Activation

Merge → rollout restart del openclaw pod. ~30s.

## Rollback

Revert commit + rollout restart.

items.title: test: isolate env mutations in root tests
items.content:
## Summary

- Replaced every legacy direct `process.env` mutation in root test files with `mockProcessEnv()` or equivalent non-mutating read patterns.
- Emptied `legacyDirectProcessEnvMutationFiles` in `test/test-hygiene.test.ts` and removed the matching Biome exclusions.
- Hardened the root hygiene guard from regex matching to TypeScript AST matching so it catches compound/logical assignments, updates, `process["env"]`, mutating `Object`/`Reflect` calls, and reference snapshots without matching comments or strings.
- Expanded the Biome Grit plugin to block alternate direct `process.env` mutation syntax, including compound/logical assignments, update operators, dynamic keys, and `delete process.env`.

## Validation

- `npm run l`
- `npm run f`
- `npx vitest run test/test-hygiene.test.ts --sequence.shuffle=false`
- `npm run tsc`
- `npm run lint:tests`
- Temporary Biome negative fixtures confirmed the plugin reports alternate mutation forms.
- `npx vitest run $(git diff --name-only --diff-filter=ACMRTUXB origin/main -- "test/**/*.test.ts" "test/*.test.ts") --sequence.shuffle=false`

All local checks passed. `npm run lint:tests` exits successfully with the existing complexity warnings in `test/assertions/meteor.test.ts` and `test/providers/google/gemini-mcp-integration.test.ts`.

items.title: feat: gate public MCP servers on explicit TLS enforcement
items.content:
## Why now
MCP security telemetry keeps converging on public-endpoint hardening (prompt injection + exposure controls + supply-chain risk), but `mcp-trust-radar` did not yet gate on transport security posture.

- Reddit: local MCP defenses against prompt injection cost/risk pressure  
  https://www.reddit.com/r/AI_Agents/comments/1rcyfey/local_mcp_that_block_prompt_injection_attacks/
- X: Wiz security guide calls out MCP prompt injection + supply chain compromise risks  
  https://x.com/wiz_io/status/2040874173824995513
- Product Hunt: multiple MCP products now market secure-by-default deployment posture  
  https://www.producthunt.com/products/mcptotal

## What shipped
- Added optional `tls_enforced` server field to model/input parsing.
- Added transport-security scoring signal:
  - public + `tls_enforced=false` => penalty
  - public + missing TLS declaration => smaller penalty
- Added release gate policy flag: `--block-public-without-tls`.
- Updated policy presets so `internet-facing` and `strict` enable TLS blocking by default.
- Added TLS details to JSON/markdown report breakdown.
- Updated example input + README docs for TLS posture.
- Added unit tests for parser, scoring, policy resolution, and gate behavior.

## Validation
### 1) Repo-level/full test suite
- `python3 -m pytest`  
  **PASS** — `46 passed, 1 warning in 0.05s`

### 2) Targeted tests for changed modules

items.title: feat(cucu-cui): Qdrant off for GUEST + identity args on CLIENT tools
items.content:
## Why

Sprint 2 prompt update paired with SaaS PR cucuapi/saas#2.

Four months of GUEST-flow hallucinations had two roots:
1. \`qdrant.search_memory\` serves 521 old WhatsApp chats with phone numbers / pushnames → the agent regurgitates them (that's how the owner's personal phone leaked).
2. \`cucu_knowledge\` has legacy \"free\"/\"gratis\" mentions that seed \"15,000 facturas gratis\" style offers.

This PR adds two hard rules at the top of AGENTS.md.

## What changed

**REGLA #0c — Qdrant PROHIBIDO para GUEST**
- If \`identify_contact → found=false\`: NO \`qdrant.*\` calls allowed.
- Answer from this prompt + \`saas.get_plans\` (real DB). That's enough for precios, demo, ventas.
- When a GUEST upgrades to CLIENT, Qdrant is re-enabled (filtered by customerId).

**REGLA #0d — Identity args on CLIENT-gated tools**
- SaaS MCP trust-gating needs to know who the end-user is per call. mcporter can't send dynamic headers, so the agent passes \`_channel\` + \`_phone\` inside tool args.
- Concrete examples for \`generate_billing_qr\`, \`emit_invoice\`, \`list_pending_collections\`.
- Missing → SaaS returns \`requires_client_level\` → operation fails cleanly.

**Three other qdrant references fixed** (REGLA #0b, CALIFICACION, CONOCIMIENTO Y MEMORIA) to clarify CLIENT-only.

## Activation

Merge → restart \`platform-openclaw\` deployment so the init-container re-clones and picks up the new AGENTS.md. Paired SaaS PR cucuapi/saas#2 already auto-injects \`_channel\`/\`_phone\` into every tool's Zod schema so the fields survive validation.

### 1. Ecosystem Pulse

The AI open-source ecosystem is undergoing a "Convergence Crunch" where the hype around agentic workflows is violently colliding with the hard reality of production stability. While the industry narrative (driven by Anthropic's official releases) pushes confidently toward autonomous software engineering, the developer community signals a warning: the tooling is breaking under the weight of rapid complexity. The single biggest story is the bifurcation of the ecosystem into high-level "Agent Orchestrators" (trending on GitHub) and low-level "Speculative Decoding" infrastructure, with the middleware layer currently experiencing a crisis of reliability.

items.title: 🛰️ AI 生态全景雷达 2026-04-18
items.content:
# AI 生态全景雷达 2026-04-18 > 数据来源: CLI 工具 + AI Agents + GitHub Trending + Hacker News + 官方内容 | 生成时间: 2026-04-18 00:14 UTC --- 你好！我是 AI 开源生态全局分析师。基于 2026-04-18 全网 5 大维度的数据源（CLI 工具链、Agent 框架、开源趋势、官方发布及 HN 社区），我为您生成了今日的**全景雷达报告**。 --- # 📡 AI 生态全景雷达报告 2026-04-18 ## 1. 生态脉搏 **关键词：工程化突围与信任危机的博弈** 今日 AI 开源生态呈现出强烈的**工程落地**与**安全反思**双重特征。一方面，生态正加速从“模型秀肌肉”转向**工具链深水区**，Anthropic 发布的 Opus 4.7 和 Claude Design 意在解决“长周期编程”与“视觉工作流”的最后几公里难题；另一方面，随着 OpenClaw 社区对恶意 Skill 的担忧及 Mythos 模型的伦理争议，**Agent 的信任与安全治理**已从理论探讨升级为 RFC 标准的紧迫需求。同时，OpenAI 的静默与其巨额硬件投资的新闻形成反差，暗示着下一波算力军备竞赛正在后台剧烈蓄力。 --- ## 2. 信号汇聚图 *以下是跨多个数据源交织出现的热点主题，代表了生态中真实的共振信号。* ### 🎨 信号 A：AI 的“视觉化”与“前端化”浪潮 * **涉及来源**：[ai-web] (Claude Design), [ai-hn] (Claude Design 讨论), [ai-trending] (前端/设计工具类增长) * **汇聚分析**：Anthropic 的 Claude Design 发布不仅是一个产品动作，更是一个生态信号。它标志着 AI 竞争已从单纯的文本/代码生成，激烈争夺**视觉产出的控制权**（UI/UX）。结合 HN 上的高热度讨论，开发者群体正迫切需要 AI 能够直接产出符合设计系统的资产，而非简单的“生成图片”。 ### 💰 信号 B：Token 经济学的焦虑与透明化 * **涉及来源**：[ai-cli] (Kimi/Codex 成本抱怨), [ai-hn] (Claude 4.7 Tokenizer 测评), [ai-web] (Opus 4.7 针对工程优化) * **汇聚分析**：从 CLI 工具用户对“Thinking 模式”烧钱的投诉，到 HN 上对 Claude 4.7 Tokenizer 效率的技术拆解，**成本控制**已成为制约 AI 落地的核心痛点。模型越强（Opus 4.7），Token 消耗越隐蔽，社区对“计费透明度”和“性价比”的敏感度达到了新高。 ### 🦠 信号 C：Agent 的“自我进化”与“失控风险” * **涉及来源**：[ai-agents] (OpenClaw 安全 RFC), [ai-trending] (GenericAgent/Evolver 项目), [ai-hn] (Mythos 伦理争议) * **汇聚分析**：Trending 上涌现出的“自我进化”类 Agent 项目与 OpenClaw 社区正在推进的“身份认证与信任验证 RFC”形成了鲜明互文。技术极客正在探索 Agent 的自主能力边界，而工程社区正在疯狂修补由此带来的安全漏洞（如 MCP 欺骗）。 ### ⚙️ 信号 D：基础设施的 Rust/Go 重构潮 * **涉及来源**：[ai-cli] (Claude Code/OpenCode 架构迁移), [ai-trending] (Google Magika 等底层工具), [ai-agents] (OpenClaw 关键修复) * **汇聚分析**：高性能语言的回归是今日 CLI 领域的暗线。Claude Code 和 OpenCode 等头部工具正试图通过抛弃 Node.js 捆绑包来解决性能与安全痛点。这表明 AI 工具链正在经历从“快速原型”向“生产级原生应用”的阵痛转型。 --- ## 3. 雷达四象限 *将今日关键信号映射到战略坐标系。* | 象限 | 状态 | 关键信号/项目 | | :--- | :--- | :--- | | **🔥 高热 / 高速** | **爆发期/舆论焦点** | **1. Claude Design (Anthropic)**：Hacker News 第一，重塑 AI 设计工作流。

items.title: fix: remediate critical vulnerabilities and logic regressions
items.content:
- [x] Mark as AI-assisted in the PR title or description
- [x] Note the degree of testing (lightly tested)
- [x] Confirm you understand what the code does

This PR fixes multiple critical bugs and vulnerabilities found during a repository audit. 🤖 **AI-assisted PR**.

## Fixes included:
1. **MCP Loopback Spoofing**: Hardcoded `senderIsOwner: false` in loopback requests.
2. **Poll Handler Race Condition**: Added inflight map deduplication cache wrapper around `sendPoll` dispatch logic.
3. **Cron Event Trust Delegation**: Added nullish coalescing default `trusted: opts?.trusted ?? true` to ensure cron-originated system events are trusted.
4. **Failover Error Classification Regression**: Moved `STATUS_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR_WITH_500_RE` check into `isTimeoutErrorMessage` and restored the correct match patterns in `isServerErrorMessage`.
5. **Delivery Cache Management Performance**: Optimized the periodic `dedupeCleanup` timer to use an iterator to drop oldest keys directly, avoiding an O(N log N) sorting spike.

items.title: feat(sanctuary-v0.10.0): WP2 Secret Broker — keychain backend + scoped ephemeral tokens + MCP interface
items.content:
## Summary

Ships Work Package 2 (Secret Broker) of the Sanctuary v0.10.0 release per [Sanctuary_V0.10.0_MultiTenancy_and_SecretBroker_Build_Plan_2026-04-17.md](../../../Code/Claude/Review/Sanctuary/Sanctuary_V0.10.0_MultiTenancy_and_SecretBroker_Build_Plan_2026-04-17.md). Three commits: broker core, policy + CLI + MCP interface, docs.

Positions Sanctuary as the open-source, MCP-native credential broker for every agent harness — not just Sanctuary-wrapped agents. Closes the gap between plaintext `.env` (what OpenClaw/ClawChief ship with today) and enterprise vaults (1Password Unified, HashiCorp Vault).

- **L3 Broker core** (`server/src/l3-disclosure/broker/`)
  - `backend-interface.ts` — pluggable Backend contract (9 methods).
  - `keychain-backend.ts` — macOS `/usr/bin/security` integration via `security -i` batch (no argv leakage). Uses a dedicated `~/Library/Keychains/sanctuary.keychain-db`, not the user's login keychain.
  - `token-issuer.ts` — per-skill scoped ephemeral tokens (default 15-min TTL, 1-hour cap), bound to `(secret, skill, scope, agent, identity_id, expires_at)`. Grant-recheck on every read.
  - `broker.ts` — orchestrator composing Backend + TokenIssuer + AuditLog.
  - `policy.ts` — policy schema validator (skills[].secrets[] with scope + ttl).
  - `open.ts` — lightweight bootstrap used by the CLI and MCP server.
- **CLI** — `sanctuary secrets {add|list|rotate|delete|grant|revoke|audit}` + `sanctuary broker-server`.
- **MCP server** (`server/src/mcp/broker-server.ts`) — four tools: `broker/request_token`, `broker/read_secret`, `broker/list_grants`, `broker/audit_query`. Denials are generic; reasons live in the audit trail only.
- **Additive to `l2-operational/audit-log.ts`** — `BROKER_OPS` constant catalog, no schema change.
- **Docs** — `server/docs/secrets.md` with quick start, security model, operational notes.
- **Test baseline** — 1463 → 1530 (+67 new tests).

### Design decisions

Two design spikes committed to `Review/Sanctuary/` before co

items.title: fix(whatsapp): wire shared defaults through accounts.default
items.content:
## Summary

- Problem: WhatsApp runtime, setup output, and security path guidance still disagreed on whether shared multi-account defaults live at channel root or under `channels.whatsapp.accounts.default`.
- Why it matters: runtime compat and setup flows were already producing `accounts.default` state in practice, but named-account resolution and user guidance did not consistently honor it.
- What changed: wired WhatsApp named accounts to inherit shared defaults from `accounts.default`, updated setup/finalize writes, and taught DM/group security path helpers to point at the effective shared default-account config.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this PR does not yet change schema validation, runtime compat tests, or live monitor debounce/self-chat follow-up behavior.

- [x] Bug fix

- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #68360
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: WhatsApp’s effective multi-account contract had drifted across runtime resolution, setup writes, and operator-facing config guidance after `accounts.default` became part of the persisted shape.
- Missing detection / guardrail: named-account resolution and config-path helper coverage did not assert `root -> accounts.default -> account` semantics.
- Contributing context (if known): runtime compat and setup flows were already generating `accounts.default`, but WhatsApp runtime was still reading only root plus the named account.

Yesterday we got not one but two big releases — a model and a harness. Neither is Mythos or
Spud, but together they represent a meaningful set of capability upgrades that will take some

OpenAI Codex for (almost) everything
Play companion site How to Use Opus 4.7 and the New Codex — companion slides
NEW RELEASES
What's New in Codex
Coding was never really the point — this makes that obvious.
The headline feature is computer use on Mac: Codex can now see, click, and type across any
app on your computer with its own cursor. Multiple agents can work in parallel in the
background without interfering with what you're doing, and Codex can operate apps that don't
have APIs. Riley Brown from Vibecode: "This is exactly what I was hoping for. Full permissions,
no Cowork-like feature limiting agent abilities. Just Codex. If you ask for a coding task, it gives
you code. If you ask for a presentation or doc, it gives you a presentation or doc." Aaron Levy
from Box sees exactly what this signals: agents that can code, work with tools, and use
computers can execute long-running tasks in the background for all areas of knowledge work —
drafting reports, setting up data rooms, reviewing contracts, processing invoices, and more. A
new in-app browser with comment mode lets

items.title: Generic OAuth2 authorization flow page
items.content:
## Summary

Add a generic OAuth2 authorization code flow to jeeves-server as a two-layer feature: a server API endpoint that constructs auth URLs and handles callbacks, plus an OpenClaw plugin tool that wraps it for conversational use.

## Motivation

Multiple Jeeves integrations require OAuth2 user tokens (X API, Google APIs, etc.). Currently each integration has its own bespoke auth script. A generic server-hosted flow eliminates the per-integration auth scaffolding and works from any browser (no localhost requirement). The plugin tool lets the agent drive the flow conversationally.

## Design

### Server API

**`POST /api/oauth/start`** (insider auth required)

Request body:
```json
{
  "provider": "x",
  "account": "karmaniverous",
  "authUrl": "https://...",
  "tokenUrl": "https://...",
  "clientId": "...",
  "clientSecret": "...",
  "scopes": "tweet.read tweet.write ...",
  "pkce": true
}
```

- If `provider` is given and matches a named provider in config, uses those defaults. Any additional fields override the named config.
- If `provider` is not given (or not found), all OAuth fields are required. This supports ad hoc authorization against unknown providers.
- Returns: `{ authUrl: string, state: string }`
- Server stores PKCE verifier + state in a short-lived in-memory map (keyed by state, TTL ~10 min).

**`GET /oauth/callback`** (no auth — this is the browser redirect target)

- Validates `state` against stored session
- Exchanges authorization `code` for tokens
- Writes tokens to `{credentialDir}/{provider}-{account}-oauth2.json`
- Renders a simple HTML page: "Authorization complete. You can close this tab."
- On error, renders an error page with details.

### Server Config

```json
{
  "oauth": {
    "credentialDir": "J:/config/credentials",
    "providers": {
      "x": {
        "authUrl": "https://twitter.com/i/oauth2/authorize",
        "tokenUrl": "https://api.x.com/2/oauth2/token",
        "pkce": true,
        "defaultScopes": "tweet.read tweet.wr

items.title: UX: misleading plugin warnings when allowlist entries don't match discovered plugin IDs
items.content:
## Problem

When a user configures `plugins.allow` with channel IDs (e.g. `"feishu"`) instead of actual plugin IDs (e.g. `"openclaw-lark"`), two confusing warnings are emitted at startup:

```
[plugins] plugins.allow is empty; discovered non-bundled plugins may auto-load: openclaw-lark (C:\Users\...\extensions\feishu-openclaw-plugin\index.js), openclaw-weixin (C:\Users\...\extensions\openclaw-weixin\index.ts). Set plugins.allow to explicit trusted ids.
[plugins] openclaw-lark: loaded without install/load-path provenance; treat as untracked local code and pin trust via plugins.allow or install records
[plugins] openclaw-weixin: loaded without install/load-path provenance; treat as untracked local code and pin trust via plugins.allow or install records
```

There are three sub-issues here:

### 1. `plugins.allow is empty` is misleading

The warning says `plugins.allow is empty` when the allowlist is **not** empty — it simply contains entries that don't match any discovered plugin IDs. The message at [`src/plugins/loader.ts:1358`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/src/plugins/loader.ts#L1358) checks `params.allow.length > 0`, which is true, but the user still sees a message implying their allowlist is empty, which is confusing.

### 2. Plugin ID vs Channel ID mismatch

The feishu channel plugin declares its plugin ID as `"openclaw-lark"` (in both `openclaw.plugin.json` and the default export), but users naturally configure it as `"feishu"` because:
- The channel config key is `channels.feishu`
- The npm package name is `feishu-openclaw-plugin`
- There is no obvious mapping from channel ID → plugin ID

This mismatch is the root cause of the warnings above. Users have no clear way to discover the actual plugin ID without reading the plugin source code or `openclaw.plugin.json`.

### 3. Unwanted plugins auto-loaded from extensions directory

Plugins in `~/.openclaw/extensions/` are auto-discovered and loaded even when the user has never configured them. In my

items.title: Competitive gap features, zero-trust security, guided CLI UX
items.content:
## Summary

Closes the top competitive gaps against Hermes Agent, OpenClaw, and Evlover while solidifying the single-host foundation with zero-trust security, pluggable storage, and a guided CLI experience.

### Competitive features (5 new grain systems)
- **Skill Memory** — agents auto-extract skills from completed tasks, retrieve them for similar future work. Pipeline integration injects matching skills into system prompts.
- **Channel Gateway** — Slack, Discord, Telegram, Teams, Email adapters with routing rules. Inbound webhook → agent → outbound response.
- **User Modeling** — cross-session user preferences, topic frequency, domain context. Automatically injected into agent prompts.
- **Curated Marketplace** — security-review-gated tool/skill registry. CLI commands: list, search, submit, publish, info.
- **Capability Templates** — versioned agent+tool configurations with validation. Publish once, instantiate across workspaces.

### Zero-trust security
- **Pluggable API auth** — `IApiAuthProvider` interface with built-in apikey/bearer providers. Enterprise extends for EntraID, Auth0, Keycloak, OIDC, SAML. Auth is a plugin (`type: "auth"`) in the workspace manifest, hot-swappable via PluginServiceBroker.
- **Audit logging** — on by default. Every API call logged: method, path, status, caller, IP, duration.
- **HTTPS enforcement** — opt-in redirect.
- **Secure connection strings** — `{provider}:{key}` references (`env:`, `file:`, `vault:`). Config never stores plaintext secrets.

### Storage backends
- **5 backends**: memory (default), SQLite, PostgreSQL, SQL Server, Redis
- **Per-workspace override** — manifest declares its own storage backend, schema, and database name
- **Schema/database isolation** — workspaces share one DB with separate schemas, or get separate databases
- **Data export/import** — `weave data export/import` for portable migration between backends
- **Safety guard** — warns if database already exists, offers stop/override/rename

### CLI UX
-

items.title: Ollama: honor Modelfile num_ctx and auto-detect tool support
items.content:
## Summary

- Problem: On a local Ollama setup, a Modelfile `PARAMETER num_ctx 32768` override was ignored during discovery (only the base model's `.context_length` was read), which tripped `context-window-guard` with "Model context window too small (8192 tokens)". Non-tool models (e.g. plain `llama3`) also hit "does not support tools" with no auto-fallback, even though Ollama already reports `capabilities` on `/api/show`.
- Why it matters: Local Ollama is a supported provider and these two gaps made common local setups (large-context custom Modelfiles, non-tool base models) fail out of the box. Issue #68344 captured both, plus a third point (`OLLAMA_API_KEY` requirement) that is already addressed at HEAD via `DEFAULT_API_KEY = "ollama-local"` / `OLLAMA_LOCAL_AUTH_MARKER` — so that part is a docs clarification only.
- What changed:
  - `queryOllamaModelShowInfo` now also parses Ollama's `parameters` string from `/api/show` and lets a positive `num_ctx <value>` override win over `model_info[*.context_length]` (last-wins, matching Ollama's own generation-time semantics).
  - `buildOllamaModelDefinition` now auto-sets `compat.supportsTools: false` when `/api/show` returns a non-empty `capabilities` array that does not include `"tools"`. Models with missing or empty capabilities keep `compat` undefined (existing permissive default).
  - Docs: added a Modelfile `num_ctx` note to the Context windows accordion, added a Tool support row to the implicit-discovery capability table, and clarified that `OLLAMA_API_KEY` is optional for local-only setups (reachable local hosts rely on the synthetic local auth marker).
  - Setup: bumped the synthetic pre-discovery capability fallback for `kimi-k2.5:cloud` from `["vision"]` to `["vision", "tools"]` so the new capability-driven compat gate does not wrongly flag Kimi as non-tool-supporting during cloud onboarding before its first `/api/show` refresh.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary):
  - No change to `OLLAMA_DEFAULT_CONTEXT_WIN

items.title: fix(whatsapp): preserve scoped group activation backfill
items.content:
## Summary

- Problem: after introducing account-scoped WhatsApp group session keys, legacy activation state still lived on the old unsuffixed key and activation-only entries could be misread or reused incorrectly.
- Why it matters: named-account group activation must survive the routing change without crashing or waking the wrong account.
- What changed: preserved legacy activation backfill into scoped keys, avoided reusing activation-only entries as full sessions, and kept default-account activation isolated from named-account backfill behavior.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this PR does not yet wire `accounts.default` shared config inheritance or schema/runtime monitor alignment.

items.title: Phase 2: Auth + Manual CRUD + 時間軸首頁 + vCard Export (Closes #4)
items.content: # Phase 2: Auth + Manual CRUD + 時間軸首頁 + vCard Export（MVP 上線點） Closes #4 ## 📋 變更摘要 **此 PR 完成 MVP**——手動輸入即可用的第一次可上線版本。合併部署後建議先用 2 週累積真實資料，再進 Phase 3 OCR。 ### 4 個 logical commits 1. **feat(phase-2): auth foundation** — middleware + Google sign-in + ALLOWED_EMAILS 白名單 + safe-action + workspace bootstrap 2. **feat(phase-2): card repository + CRUD + CardForm** — Firestore Admin 讀寫層 + 3 個 Server Actions + RHF + Zod 表單 3. **feat(phase-2): list + timeline home + detail + vCard export** — 畫廊/清單雙模式、**差異化時間軸首頁**、magazine 詳細頁、vCard 4.0 匯出 4. **test(phase-2): unit + E2E** — 39 單元測試 + 5 E2E + 清理舊 Phase 1 佔位 ## ✅ Acceptance Criteria（對應 #4） - [x] 只有 `ALLOWED_EMAILS` 白名單可登入（`createSession` 驗、E2E 驗未登入 redirect） - [x] Personal workspace 首次登入自動建立（`ensurePersonalWorkspace` idempotent） - [x] 手動表單建卡 → 列表可見 → 詳細頁可讀（全流程 Server Components + Server Actions） - [x] 「為什麼記得這個人」必填欄位 + 必填視覺標示 + Zod refine - [x] vCard 4.0 匯出：iOS 可匯入（已含 X-FIRST-MET + NOTE 保留「為什麼記得」） - [x] 時間軸首頁 3 區塊：新建立 / 本月認識 / 最近沒聯絡 - [x] 響應式 320 / 768 / 1440 無 overflow（grid auto-fill + media queries） - [x] Coverage ≥ 80%（排除 firebase/** 與 rules test，剩餘 100%） - [x] 設計自檢「能放進 Kinfolk 嗎？」—— Fraunces 變體字 + Noto Serif TC italic + 朱紅單一 accent ## 🏗️ 差異化核心：時間軸首頁 首頁**不是**名片列表。是「今天該問候誰？」的人脈節奏表： | 區塊 | 規則 | Sort | |------|------|------| | **新建立** | `createdAt` 最近 7 天 | createdAt desc | | **本月認識** | `firstMetDate` 在本月 | firstMetDate desc | | **最近沒聯絡** | `lastContactedAt` 超過 30 天，或無 lastContactedAt 且 createdAt 超過 30 天 | 最久沒聯絡排前面 | 這是對抗 iOS 17+ 內建名片掃描的護城河——`categorizeTimeline` 12 個 unit tests 鎖定分類邏輯不回溯。 ## 🎨 設計亮點 - **登入頁**：Fraunces italic 「登入你的名片冊」，Google 按鈕是 paper 背景 + hair border + 1px hover lift - **時間軸**：「今天 今天 該問候誰？」— em 是朱紅 italic Noto Serif TC，tracking-tight 拉緊 - **畫廊**：每張卡有基於 id 的 deterministic ±1° tilt，hover 解除回正 + scale 1.02。每第 7 張 span 2 column 打破均勻感（參考 Kinfolk 編排） - **詳細頁**：大字名字 + italic English 副 + "Role 於 Company" — 「於」是 italic 朱紅分隔；why-remember 是 accent-soft pull-quote - **CardActions**：sidebar 垂直 stac

items.title: feat: add /ghcp-handoff skill for bounded copilot delegation
items.content:
## Summary

Adds a new sibling skill `/ghcp-handoff` next to `/codex`. Where `/codex` pulls a second opinion in, `/ghcp-handoff` pushes bounded mechanical work out — scaffolds, CI wiring, mechanical refactors, test generation — to the GitHub Copilot CLI (`copilot`) while the user keeps judgment calls in Claude.

**Three modes:**
- **Guided (default)** — interactive AskUserQuestion flow collects task summary, target branch, context files, streams, hard boundaries, NOT-in-scope paths, forbidden deps, and PR sections, renders a prompt from `templates/prompt-template.md`, and invokes `copilot -p` non-interactively with a 30-minute timeout.
- **`--from-plan <path>`** — extracts the `## The GHCP prompt` section from a plan file, wraps it with a worktree instruction and embedded metadata block, then invokes copilot.
- **`verify <pr>`** — resolves the PR to a branch via `gh pr view`, locates the matching prompt file under `.gstack/ghcp-handoff/`, parses the `<!-- ghcp-handoff:metadata -->` YAML, and runs three checks: boundary diff (changed files vs NOT-in-scope globs), dependency deviation (new deps flagged, forbidden deps blocked), and PR body contract (required sections present). Writes a gstack-review-log JSONL entry so results surface in the Plan Status Footer. Exit code 2 is reserved for metadata parse failures so they're distinct from violations found.

**Safety:**
- Target branch auto-derives `ghcp/<slug>` and hard-rejects `main`, `master`, `dev`, `develop`, `production`, `staging`.
- Prompt instructs copilot to use a clean git worktree so parallel agents don't collide.
- Degrades gracefully if `copilot` isn't installed — writes the prompt file, tells the user where to paste it.

**Structure** follows the `/browse` pattern (co-located TS + tests):
```
ghcp-handoff/
├── SKILL.md               (generated via bun run gen:skill-docs)
├── SKILL.md.tmpl          (uses {{PREAMBLE}} + {{BASE_BRANCH_DETECT}} resolvers)
├── bin/
│   ├── ghcp-detect.sh
│   ├── ghcp-extract-pla

items.title: [Repo Assist] improve: modernize Substring→range syntax; consolidate TruncateLabel duplicate
items.content:
🤖 *This is an automated PR from Repo Assist.*

## Summary

Two related clean-up improvements to `OpenClaw.Shared`:

### 1. Modernise `Substring(0, n)` → C# range syntax `[..n]`

Replaces all `str.Substring(0, n)` / `str.Substring(0, Math.Min(n, len))` calls in the shared library with the equivalent C# 8+ range syntax:

| File | Old | New |
|---|---|---|
| `DeviceIdentity.cs` | `_deviceId?.Substring(0, 16)` | `_deviceId?[..16]` |
| `CanvasCapability.cs` | `script.Substring(0, Math.Min(50, script.Length))` | `script[..Math.Min(50, script.Length)]` |
| `OpenClawGatewayClient.cs` (×3) | `text.Substring(0, Math.Min(n, text.Length))` | `text[..Math.Min(n, text.Length)]` |
| `WindowsNodeClient.cs` (×4) | `.Substring(0, 16)` / `.Substring(0, 50)` | `[..16]` / `[..50]` |

### 2. Remove duplicate `TruncateLabel` private method

`OpenClawGatewayClient` contained a private `TruncateLabel` helper whose logic was byte-for-byte identical to the public `MenuDisplayHelper.TruncateText`. The three call sites are updated to call `MenuDisplayHelper.TruncateText(..., 60)` directly, and the reflection-based test helper in `OpenClawGatewayClientTests` is updated to use the public API instead of reflection.

No behaviour change — the two implementations produced identical output for all inputs.

## Test Status

All tests pass after the changes:

- **Shared.Tests**: 586 passed, 20 skipped (606 total)
- **Tray.Tests**: 122 passed

Implements **The Watcher of Claw** upstream monitoring for the OpenClaw fork.

items.title: [Bug]: Ollama integration issues: context window ignored + tool support blocking local models
items.content:
### Bug type

Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash)

### Beta release blocker

No

### Summary

Hi OpenClaw team,

I wanted to report a set of issues I encountered while trying to use OpenClaw with a local Ollama setup. I’m documenting this step-by-step so it’s easy to reproduce.

Environment
macOS (Apple Silicon)
OpenClaw v2026.4.15
Ollama (local, default port 127.0.0.1:11434)
Model: llama3 (custom variant with increased context)
Issue 1 — Context window mismatch

I created a custom Ollama model with:

FROM llama3:latest
PARAMETER num_ctx 32768

Verified in Ollama:

ollama run llama3-32k:latest
/show info
→ context length: 32768

However, OpenClaw still reports:

openclaw models list
→ ollama/llama3-32k:latest … 8k

And the agent fails with:

Model context window too small (8192 tokens). Minimum is 16000.

This suggests OpenClaw is not detecting or respecting the overridden num_ctx value from Ollama.

Issue 2 — Ollama provider requires API key

OpenClaw would not properly use Ollama until I set:

export OLLAMA_API_KEY="ollama-local"

This is confusing because:

Ollama is local and does not require authentication
The error message implies authentication is required

It would help if this were either:

optional, or
clearly documented as a registration flag rather than actual authentication
Issue 3 — Tool support blocking usage

After resolving context issues, I encountered:

does not support tools

This prevents using the model in the default chat/agent UI.

From what I can tell:

OpenClaw assumes tool-capable models
Ollama models like llama3 do not support tools
There is no fallback to plain text / non-tool mode

This effectively blocks using OpenClaw with local models for simple generation tasks.

Overall impact

At the moment, a local Ollama workflow in OpenClaw is difficult because:

Context overrides are not recognized
Provider requires a dummy API key
Non-tool models are rejected by the agent system

This makes it hard to use OpenClaw for basic lo

items.title: 📰 科研 & 技术热点日报 - 2026-04-18 上午
items.content:
# 🧠 科研 & 技术热点日报

日期：2026-04-18 上午
生成时间：2026/04/18 08:30

## 📝 今日总结

# 2026年4月18日 科研与技术热点新闻总结

## 1. 今日最重要的技术趋势和热点

**（1）智能体（Agent）的“探索-利用”平衡进入可量化评估时代**  
今日多篇论文（如arXiv:2604.13151）聚焦于**语言模型智能体在开放环境中的决策能力评估**。研究者设计了可控的2D网格环境与任务DAG（有向无环图），通过程序化调节地图生成难度，首次实现了**在不访问智能体内部策略的情况下，从外部行为数据中量化其“探索”与“利用”错误**。这标志着智能体评估从黑箱走向可解释、可度量，为后续优化提供了关键基准。

**（2）面向科学研究的“安全、轻量、自主”智能体工作流兴起**  
SciFi框架（arXiv:2604.13180）提出了一种**三层智能体循环+自评估“执行-直到”机制**，结合隔离执行环境，旨在实现科学任务的端到端自动化。其核心创新在于**将任务限定在结构化、有明确停止条件的范围内**，从而在保证安全性的前提下，利用不同能力层级的LLM完成实验设计、数据分析等流程。这反映了AI科研正从“辅助工具”向“自主实验者”演进。

**（3）多模态大模型训练进入“数据配方”优化新阶段**  
MixAtlas方法（arXiv:2604.14198）首次将多模态训练数据分解为**视觉概念（10个CLIP聚类）与任务类型（5类监督目标）两个维度**，通过高斯过程代理模型与主动学习，为特定下游任务自动寻找最优数据混合比例。这表明单纯堆砌数据量的时代正在过去，**“高质量、结构化、目标导向”的数据混合策略**成为提升多模态模型性能的关键杠杆。

**（4）RAG技术向知识图谱与合规性双重演进**  
今日研究显示，RAG正从简单的向量检索升级为**知识图谱增强的智能体化爬取与构建系统**（arXiv:2604.14220），在复杂法规查询中比传统向量检索准确率提升70%。同时，产业界开始关注**RAG数据管道的合规风险审计**（InformationWeek），提示企业需警惕检索内容的知识产权、隐私与事实准确性隐患。RAG进入“精度+可信度”双轮驱动阶段。

**（5）大模型推理与压缩技术走向动态自适应**  
新研究（arXiv:2604.14156）提出**压缩感知引导的动态结构化稀疏框架**，能够根据输入提示与解码步骤动态选择激活的子网络，实现“**推理时自适应压缩**”。这打破了传统静态模型压缩的局限，让模型在不同任务情境下自动调整计算路径，为边缘部署与低延迟推理开辟新路径。

---

## 2. 值得关注的研究方向或突破

- **梯度纠缠（Gradient Entanglement）的发现与解决**：在广义类别发现（GCD）任务中，研究者定量分析了**监督与无监督目标联合优化时梯度相互干扰的问题**，并提出能量感知梯度协调器（EAGC）进行解耦（arXiv:2604.14176）。这一发现可能影响所有多目标学习的模型训练范式。
- **长时记忆评估的游戏化基准MemGround**：该工作（arXiv:2604.14158）通过**三层记忆框架（表面状态、时序关联、推理记忆）** 在交互式游戏场景中系统评估LLM记忆能力，突破了传统静态问答评估的局限，为具身智能与持续学习奠定基础。
- **分布式DiT推理的通信优化**：CoCoDiff（arXiv:2604.14561）针对Ulysses序列并行中的All-to-All通信瓶颈，利用**Q/K与V计算路径差异与相邻去噪步的时序局部性**，实现了通信与计算的高效重叠，对大规模扩散模型推理加速有重要工程价值。
- **基于eBPF的部署安全增强**：GitHub工程团队分享如何利用**eBPF技术实时检测并防止部署过程中的循环依赖**，这是云原生时代保障关键系统可用性的前沿实践。

---

## 3. 简要分析与展望

今日新闻整体呈现 **“AI系统从能力演示走向可靠部署”** 的鲜明趋势。无论是智能体的可量化评估、科学工作流的安全隔离，还是RAG的合规审查，都指向同一个方向：**AI正在从实验室原型加速渗透到生产环境，而可靠性、安全性与可解释性成为技术演进的核心约束**。

另一个值得注意的动向是 **“垂直整合”** ：Codex新增计算机使用、浏览、图像生成等多模态能力，正在从一个代码助手演变为**全栈开发者工作流操作系统**；Claude Code（arXiv:2604.14228）等智能体系统则通过清晰的架构设计原则（人类决策权威、安全可靠等），将AI能力深度嵌入到软件工程生命周期中。

展望未来，我们

items.title: audit: land P0/P1 remediation from 2026-04-17 vibe-code-audit
items.content:
## Summary

Audit remediation for the 2026-04-17 vibe-code-audit (see `docs/audit/2026-04-17/AUDIT.md`). Takes the production VM from crash-looping to serving cleanly, moves secrets out of the systemd unit, closes the P0/P1 audit findings, and centralizes external-service configuration.

Branch: `audit/2026-04-17-fixes` → `main`
Rollback anchor: tag `pre-audit-fixes` on the pre-fix HEAD (`ea3b0fd`).

## What changed on the running VM (not in this diff)

- `~/.config/systemd/user/clawtrol.service` — `Type=forking` → `Type=simple`, dropped `-d`, removed `PIDFile=` / `ExecStop=` (crash loop), added `EnvironmentFile=/etc/clawtrol/clawtrol.env`.
- `/etc/clawtrol/clawtrol.env` — new file, `0640 root:ggorbalan`, holds `SECRET_KEY_BASE` (rotated), `DATABASE_URL`, `HOOKS_TOKEN` (was unset in prod — caused every boot to log `[SECURITY]`), `CLAWTROL_API_TOKEN`, `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_URL`, `DOCS_HUB_URL`, `QDRANT_URL`, `OLLAMA_URL`, `APP_BASE_URL`, plus a `DATABASE_ADMIN_URL` for migrations.
- Postgres role `clawtrol_app` created with DML-only grants; DB superuser (`postgres`) retained only for `DATABASE_ADMIN_URL`.
- Ran `db:migrate` (2 new migrations landed) + `db:schema:load:cache` (solid_cache_entries was never loaded — caused 500s on anything touching the cache).

## What changed in this diff

### Fix the crash loop (Phase 1)

- **`docker-compose.yml`** — expose Postgres on host `127.0.0.1:15432`. Before: no port binding → Rails (host-side) couldn't reach the DB → SolidQueue crashed on boot → Puma died → systemd restart-looped.

### Critical audit findings closed (Phases 3 + 4)

- **`app/controllers/api/v1/pipeline_controller.rb`** — inherit `BaseController`, remove local `authenticate_user!` that silently fell back to `User.where(admin: true).first || User.first`. Closes audit FIX #3.
- **`app/controllers/concerns/api/webhook_idempotency.rb`** (new) + **`hooks_controller.rb`** + **`nightshift_controller.rb`** — dedup webhooks by `X-Hook-Event-Id` via existing `WebhookLog` mod

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

If this PR fixes a plugin beta-release blocker, title it `fix(<plugin-id>): beta blocker - <summary>` and link the matching `Beta blocker: <plugin-name> - <summary>` issue labeled `beta-blocker`. Contributors cannot label PRs, so the title is the PR-side signal for maintainers and automation.

- Problem: `extensions/nvidia/index.ts` declared `auth: []`, which excluded NVIDIA from the `openclaw onboard` auth-choice prompt even though the plugin already had `envVars` and a model catalog wired up.

items.title: telegram: add allowBots parity for inbound filtering
items.content:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

**Telegram bots forwarding messages into configured channels were processed as normal user messages, creating noise, potential loops, and unintended AI responses to automated traffic.**

If this PR fixes a plugin beta-release blocker, title it `fix(<plugin-id>): beta blocker - <summary>` and link the matching `Beta blocker: <plugin-name> - <summary>` issue labeled `beta-blocker`. Contributors cannot label PRs, so the title is the PR-side signal for maintainers and automation.

- Problem:
- Why it matters:
Without a bot gate, any bot posting in a monitored Telegram group triggers the full OpenClaw message pipeline — responses, memory writes, skill execution — on non-human input.

- What changed:

Added allowBots?: boolean to the Telegram channel config type, Zod schema, UI hints, and label/help metadata.
Added a bot-message drop gate in bot-handlers.runtime.ts: if the inbound message is authored by a bot (from.is_bot === true) and allowBots is not true, the message is logged and discarded before pipeline entry.
Converted two Telegram test files from await import(...) to static imports; refactored shared test harness to export an explicit resetTelegramHarnessStateForTest() function instead of a module-level beforeEach.
Added a negative test: "drops bot-authored channel_post messages when allowBots is not enabled".


- What did NOT change (scope boundary):

No changes to any other channel (Slack, Discord, etc.), the gateway/orchestration layer, memory, auth, or CI config.
Global Vitest test infrastructure (test/non-isolated-runner.ts, test/setup-openclaw-runtime.ts, test/setup.extensions.ts) was not modified; a pre-existing TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'config') regression in the custom runner is unrelated to this PR.

## Change Type (select all)

- [*] Bug fix
- [*] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Ch

items.title: Fix macos ssh host checking
items.content:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

If this PR fixes a plugin beta-release blocker, title it `fix(<plugin-id>): beta blocker - <summary>` and link the matching `Beta blocker: <plugin-name> - <summary>` issue labeled `beta-blocker`. Contributors cannot label PRs, so the title is the PR-side signal for maintainers and automation.

- Problem: macOS remote SSH command, probe, tunnel, and pairing checks silently trusted first-seen host keys by using `StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new`.
- Why it matters: first-connect remote sessions could establish trust without a pre-trusted `known_hosts` entry, weakening SSH host-authentication posture on the macOS remote path.
- What changed: centralized the strict host-key SSH option fragments in `CommandResolver`, switched the four macOS SSH call sites to `StrictHostKeyChecking=yes`, and added a regression assertion in `CommandResolverTests`.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no auth flow changes, no SSH identity selection changes, and no broader remote-mode refactor beyond the host-key policy.

items.author: drobison00

items.title: [NemoClaw][CLI] nemoclaw policy-add has no custom-preset surface; custom egress targets require dropping to openshell policy set
items.content:
`nemoclaw policy-add` ships with 11 built-in preset names (brave, discord, telegram, claude_code, clawhub, npm_registry, nvidia, openclaw_api, openclaw_docs, statsig, sentry). There is no flag, path argument, or config surface that accepts a user-authored preset YAML.

If an operator has a custom egress target, for example a self-hosted SearXNG at `http://192.168.1.105:8080`, the supported NemoClaw commands offer nowhere to land it. The next step is to drop one layer down to `openshell policy set my-assistant --policy merged.yaml`, which the NemoClaw quickstart warnings call out as not recommended on NemoClaw-managed sandboxes.

This means non-default deployments (enterprise metasearch, internal APIs, private registries, custom inference targets) need to bypass the NemoClaw surface for egress policy.

### Environment

- NemoClaw v0.0.17, OpenShell v0.0.26
- Sandbox: `my-assistant` on DietPi2 (x86_64, 64GB, MicroK8s)
- Use case: allow the agent to reach an internal SearXNG instance for the `web-search` skill

### Steps to reproduce

```bash
# What the operator wants:
nemoclaw my-assistant policy-add --preset-file ./presets/websearch.yaml
# → no such flag

nemoclaw my-assistant policy-add websearch
# → Error: unknown preset "websearch"

nemoclaw my-assistant policy-add --help
# → shows only the 11 built-in names; no file input; no --dry-run for custom YAML

# What actually works (workaround):
openshell policy get my-assistant --full > /tmp/current.yaml
# hand-strip the 5-line banner ("Version:", "Hash:", etc.) from the top of the file
sed -n "/^version: 1/,$p" /tmp/current.yaml > /tmp/clean.yaml
cat my-websearch-block.yaml >> /tmp/clean.yaml
openshell policy set my-assistant --policy /tmp/clean.yaml
```

This does work (SearXNG became reachable from the sandbox), but it has three concerns:

1. `openshell policy get --full` emits a 5-line status banner that is not valid YAML; callers have to know to strip it.
2. There is no round-trip primitive: no `get --yaml-only`,

items.title: Possible complementary direction: SkillClaw as a long-term skill lifecycle layer
items.content:
Hi, I'd like to suggest a complementary direction for `claude-workflow-v2`.

Project:
https://github.com/AMAP-ML/SkillClaw

Your project focuses on workflow-oriented skill / agent automation tooling.

SkillClaw already supports Hermes, OpenClaw, and other OpenAI-compatible agent setups. Its focus is not the interactive surface itself, but what happens after repeated use: skill libraries become duplicated, stale, and fragmented over time.

It adds a post-task skill evolution loop that:

- deduplicates overlapping skills
- merges related skills
- improves skill quality over time
- shares evolved skills across agents / devices / teams

I think that makes it complementary to alternative runtimes and harnesses: users keep their current runtime, while SkillClaw acts as the long-term skill lifecycle layer.

If useful, I can put together a concise demo or integration note.

Paper:
https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.08377

items.author: lilubot

items.title: Add `openclaw sessions send` CLI command to expose session messaging
items.content:
## Problem

- `chain-runner` assumes `openclaw sessions send` exists
- It does not
- Current dispatch silently no-ops
- `team-*` chains therefore fail to wake downstream agents unless manually nudged via MCP `sessions_send`

The Discord self-post fallback some chains use isn't a real dispatch mechanism — it masks the underlying bug rather than fixing it.

## Requested command

```
openclaw sessions send --key <sessionKey> --message <message>
```

Ideally also:
- `--label <label>`
- `--timeout <seconds>`

## Why

- Parity with MCP `sessions_send`
- Enables automation from bash/python
- Fixes chain dispatch and watch handoffs
- Removes brittle Discord self-post fallback
- Helps every future `team-*` chain, not just current runs

## Nice to have

- Non-zero exit on delivery failure
- Clear stderr if target session is missing/unreachable
- Support stdin for message body

items.title: fix(openrouter): surface reasoning_details visible-text as user output (#68261)
items.content:
## Summary

- Problem: OpenRouter model runs (e.g. `openrouter/google/gemini-2.5-flash`, Grok 4.x, minimax-m2.7) return blank responses with `stopReason=stop payloads=0` and surface `⚠️ Agent couldn't generate a response`.
- Why it matters: Entire classes of OpenRouter-proxied models have been unusable since 2026.4.14/15 despite healthy gateway and successful upstream responses.
- What changed: `getCompletionsReasoningDelta` in `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts` now discriminates the OpenRouter `reasoning_details` stream between hidden reasoning (`reasoning.text`) and OpenAI Responses–style visible output (`response.output_text`, `response.text`, `text`). Visible-output items are routed through the same text-block path as `choice.delta.content` instead of being buried in a thinking block.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no changes to prompts, auth, sandbox, tool policy, secret handling, provider routing, request assembly, transcript bytes, or prompt-cache order. Previous `reasoning.text` behavior is preserved byte-for-byte.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #68261
- Related #67410, #67575, #67698, #67907, #67425, #68030, #68076, #68185
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause

`e0bf756b50` ("fix: handle OpenRouter Qwen3 reasoning_details streams") taught `getCompletionsReasoningDelta` to parse `reasoning_details` items, but it unconditionally classified every matching item as hidden thinking. Some OpenRouter upstreams (Gemini 2.5 Flash via the Responses-style reasoning proxy, xAI Grok 4.x, and related models) deliver the assistant's final answer inside `reasoning_details` using OpenAI R

items.title: Compaction loop: calculateContextTokens crashes on missing usage.input (Anthropic OAuth path)
items.content:
## Bug Description

During compaction, `completeSimple(...)` returns a response whose `usage` field is missing or partial, causing `calculateContextTokens(usage)` to crash with:

```
Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'input')
```

In the gateway log this surfaces as:

```
[compaction] Full summarization failed: Summarization failed: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'input')
[agent/embedded] embedded run agent end: ... isError=true model=claude-opus-4-7 provider=anthropic-oauth
  error=Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'input')
```

The session then cannot make progress — every follow-up run triggers another compaction attempt that fails with the same error, producing a loop of ~10-15 failed runs until a gateway restart clears it.

## Reproduction

Observed on 2026-04-18 with OpenClaw 2026.4.15 on macOS arm64.

Sequence:
1. Persistent agent session (`agent:system-architect:telegram:direct:<id>`) accumulates large context from a long planning task (multiple tool calls, mixed Anthropic + OpenAI routes).
2. Context-overflow guard triggers auto-compaction with `anthropic-oauth/claude-opus-4-7`.
3. `generateSummary` in `pi-coding-agent/dist/core/compaction/compaction.js` calls `completeSimple(...)`. The call returns, but somewhere between the return and the next `calculateContextTokens(response.usage)` the `usage` object (or at least `usage.input`) is undefined.
4. First occurrence: `07:27:30`. Identical error repeats: `07:27:38`, `07:27:41`, `07:37:42`, `07:38:24`, `07:44:43`, `08:01:40`, `08:03:56`, `08:04:04`, `08:06:56`, `08:07:13`, `08:07:23` — 12 failed runs in ~40 minutes before the user noticed.
5. Gateway restart at `08:12` cleared the loop; no recurrence afterward.

## Root Cause (best guess)

`core/compaction/compaction.js:79`:

```js
export function calculateContextTokens(usage) {
    return usage.totalTokens || usage.input + usage.output + usage.cacheRead + usage.cacheWrite;
}
```

If `usage` itself or `usage.input` is undefi

items.title: fix(k8s): interpose docker socket proxy between workspace and daemon
items.content:
## Summary

- Adds a `tecnativa/docker-socket-proxy:0.6.0` sidecar that filters Docker API calls from workspace to the rootless DinD daemon
- Removes direct docker-socket volume mount from workspace — it now talks to the proxy over `tcp://localhost:2375`
- Proxy denies exec, build, swarm, plugin, secret, and other dangerous endpoints; allows only containers, images, networks, volumes, info, and version
- Proxy runs as nobody (UID 65534) with GID 1000 (matching pod fsGroup) for non-root socket access
- Socket volume mounted at dedicated `/docker-socket` path with `readOnly: true` to avoid HAProxy runtime conflicts
- Extends Docker readiness timeout from 60s to 90s for rootless DinD + proxy startup
- Updates README: architecture diagram, security defaults list, prerequisites, and proxy troubleshooting section
- Adds security test assertions for proxy hardening and workspace socket isolation

### Depends on

- #2044 (rootless DinD) — first commit squashes that PR as the base; rebase after merge

### What this blocks

| Attack | Before | After |
|--------|--------|-------|
| `docker exec` into inner containers | Works | Blocked (EXEC=0) |
| `docker build` with malicious Dockerfile | Works | Blocked (BUILD=0) |
| Swarm/plugin/secret/config operations | Works | Blocked (denied by default) |
| Host-path bind mount escape | Blocked by rootless DinD | Double-blocked (rootless + no raw socket) |

### Volume mount picture

| Volume | dind | docker-proxy | workspace |
|--------|------|--------------|-----------|
| docker-socket | read-write | read-only | not mounted |
| docker-storage | read-write | not mounted | not mounted |
| docker-config | read-write | not mounted | not mounted |

## Test plan

- [x] YAML validates (`python3 -c "import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open(...))"`)
- [x] Security hardening tests pass (2/2) — existing + new proxy assertions
- [ ] Deploy to K8s cluster, confirm all three containers reach Running
- [ ] `kubectl exec -it nemoclaw -c workspace -- docker in

items.title: fix(kilocode): send auth header in model discovery to include BYOK models
items.content:
## Summary
- Send `KILOCODE_API_KEY` as a Bearer token in the **extension** kilocode model discovery fetch so the gateway can resolve the user and include their BYOK models in the response.

## Problem
When OpenClaw discovers models via `fetch(KILOCODE_MODELS_URL)` in `extensions/kilocode/provider-models.ts`, it sends no auth header. The gateway (`api.kilo.ai/api/gateway/models`) only appends BYOK models when it can identify the user. Without auth, BYOK models are silently dropped, making BYOK providers like `zai-coding` non-functional in Kiloclaw.

## Scope note
PR #32352 introduced dynamic model discovery in **two** code paths:
- **`src/agents/kilocode-models.ts`** — core path (used by standard OpenClaw)
- **`extensions/kilocode/provider-models.ts`** — extension path (used by Kiloclaw)

This PR only fixes the **extension path**. The core path still has the same unauthenticated fetch, so #65578 (the regression affecting standard OpenClaw users like SergioNR) will require a separate fix to `src/agents/kilocode-models.ts`.

## Why this works
- `KILOCODE_API_KEY` is already in the environment (Kiloclaw passes it as an env var)
- It's a JWT with `kiloUserId` and `version: 3` — the exact format the gateway already validates
- The gateway's `getUserFromAuth()` already reads `Authorization: Bearer` headers and resolves users
- **Zero changes needed in Kilo-Org/cloud**

## Backward compatibility
- No `KILOCODE_API_KEY` → no `Authorization` header → same behavior as today
- Test environment returns static catalog before fetch is called (existing tests unaffected)
- Non-Kiloclaw OpenClaw instances are unaffected
- Trims env vars before use to handle stray whitespace/newlines (review feedback from #66175)

Recreated from a clean branch after #66175 was closed for picking up unrelated commits during a rebase.

Related openclaw/openclaw#65578 (extension path only; core path still unfixed)
Related Kilo-Org/cloud#2384

🤖 AI-assisted (Kilo with GLM-5.1). Fully reviewed and underst

items.title: Design culture.dev positioning against Codex, Claude Code, and OpenClaw
items.content:
## Summary
Design how `https://culture.dev` introduces Culture relative to existing agent/code tools, especially Codex, Claude Code, and OpenClaw.

## Context
The current mental model can be misread as contrasting Culture with a stateless or one-shot AI interface. That is too weak and can lead to bad summaries.

Codex, Claude Code, and OpenClaw already support persistence/teachability in different ways:
- saving docs and workspace knowledge
- reusing file-backed context
- maintaining memory over time

OpenClaw is an especially relevant comparison because it explicitly maintains memory/identity in files.

## Goal
Make the site explain that Culture is **not** mainly about whether agents can remember or be taught.

The stronger distinction is that Culture treats agents as members of a shared social/runtime system:
- named members
- presence
- rooms/channels
- roles
- lifecycle (`introduce -> educate/teach -> join -> mentor -> promote`)
- federation / multi-machine collaboration

## Desired outcome
Create or revise site content so readers come away with something closer to:

> Codex, Claude Code, and OpenClaw can all be taught and can persist knowledge.
> Culture's contribution is the shared protocol + social structure around human-agent collaboration.

Or even more specifically:

> OpenClaw externalizes memory and identity into files.
> Culture is differentiated less by persistence itself and more by shared presence, roles, coordination, and culture-level organization.

## Proposed work
- Review `vision`, `mental model`, and related landing-page copy on `culture.dev`
- Add a section comparing Culture to existing coding agents
- Avoid metaphors like "vending machine" that imply other agents are stateless
- Introduce a stronger comparison point using OpenClaw
- Clarify that the "teach" phase is real and important, but not unique to Culture
- Emphasize that Culture connects teachability to membership, coordination, and ongoing social structure

-

items.author: Copilot

items.title: Native Aero agent loop: rip out OpenClaw, ship built-in agent with dashboard bar
items.content:
## Summary

Rips out the OpenClaw-backed agent runtime and ships **Aero**, a built-in AI agent powered by [`@mariozechner/pi-agent-core`](https://github.com/badlogic/pi-mono). Adds a CLI (`canonry agent ask`), a dashboard command bar, proactive wake-up on run completion, and 13 typed tools.

Users with their own agent (Claude Code, Codex, custom) drive canonry through the regular CLI/API — same surface as any human operator. `canonry agent attach --url …` is a **separate** concern: it registers a push-notification webhook for agents that want to be woken up by `run.completed` / insight events instead of polling. It's only needed for long-lived agent services that listen on a webhook endpoint; interactive agents can ignore it entirely.

Net change: **+~3.4k / -~3k lines** across 46 files. Detailed commit log below.

## Motivation

OpenClaw introduced a separate process, custom install flow (`canonry agent setup`), and a pinned external dependency tree that didn't match canonry's "agent-first, CLI-native" direction. The native path:

- No separate process — Aero lives inside `canonry serve`.
- No install dance — `canonry agent ask <project> "…"` works as soon as an API key for any supported LLM is configured.
- First-class streaming, parallel tool execution, and an extensible tool registry courtesy of `pi-agent-core`.
- Hybrid persistence: live `Agent` instance in-memory per project, durable transcript + follow-up queue in a new `agent_sessions` table.

## What's in the box

### CLI — built-in Aero

```bash
canonry agent ask <project> "<prompt>" [--provider anthropic|openai|google|zai] [--model <id>] [--format json]
```

Conversations persist per project — subsequent `agent ask` invocations continue the same rolling thread.

### CLI — external-agent push webhook (unchanged, just re-housed)

```bash
canonry agent attach <project> --url <webhook-url>
canonry agent detach <project>
```

`attach` registers a notification row (`source: 'agent'`) subscribed to `run.complete
items.author: arberx

items.title: feat: make PROVIDER_TIMEOUT_MS configurable via environment variable
items.content:
## Summary

Fixes #1583

- Replaces the hardcoded `180_000` ms timeout in `provider-client.ts` with a value read from `process.env.PROVIDER_TIMEOUT_MS`, falling back to `180_000` if unset or invalid.

## Problem

With matching 180s timeouts between Manifest and upstream clients (e.g. OpenClaw), both fire simultaneously. The client disconnects first, causing Manifest to see `signal.aborted = true` from the client disconnect and re-throw instead of triggering the fallback chain.

## Solution

```typescript
const PROVIDER_TIMEOUT_MS = parseInt(process.env.PROVIDER_TIMEOUT_MS ?? '', 10) || 180_000;
```

Self-hosted users can now set a lower timeout in `docker-compose.yml`:

```yaml
environment:
  PROVIDER_TIMEOUT_MS: 45000
```

With 45s per attempt: primary (45s) + fallback 1 (45s) + fallback 2 (45s) = 135s — well within a typical 300s upstream timeout, allowing the fallback chain to complete.

## Test plan

- [ ] No `PROVIDER_TIMEOUT_MS` env var set → timeout defaults to `180_000`
- [ ] `PROVIDER_TIMEOUT_MS=45000` → timeout uses `45000`
- [ ] `PROVIDER_TIMEOUT_MS=abc` (invalid) → timeout falls back to `180_000`

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---
## Summary by cubic
Make the provider request timeout configurable via the `PROVIDER_TIMEOUT_MS` environment variable instead of a fixed 180s. Defaults to 180000 ms if unset or invalid, allowing fallback chains to complete before upstream client timeouts.

<sup>Written for commit 6a1a4f4e122ef24d53702242b25cca928273719d. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->

items.title: [Bug]: Schema .strict() rejects `paperclip` property from Paperclip 2026.416.0 wake payload
items.content:
### Bug type

Regression (worked before, now fails)

### Beta release blocker

No

### Summary

## Summary

After upgrading Paperclip to `2026.416.0`, all `openclaw_gateway` adapter runs fail with:

`invalid agent params: at root: unexpected property 'paperclip'`

Paperclip now injects a `paperclip: {...}` object into its wake payload, but OpenClaw's agent schema uses Zod's `.strict()` mode which rejects any unknown property.

## Environment

| Component | Version |
|---|---|
| OpenClaw | `2026.4.15 (041266a)` |
| Paperclip | `2026.416.0` |
| Node.js | `v22.22.2` |
| OS | Windows 11 + WSL2 (Ubuntu 24.04.3 LTS) |
| Install path | `/home/elban/.npm-global/lib/node_modules/openclaw` |
| Channel | Tested on both `stable` and `beta` — same version, same failure |

## Reproduction steps

1. Install Paperclip `2026.416.0` on Windows (via `npx paperclipai onboard`)
2. Install OpenClaw `2026.4.15` in WSL2 (via `curl -fsSL https://openclaw.ai/install.sh | bash`)
3. In Paperclip, create an agent with adapter type `openclaw_gateway`
4. Configure gateway URL (`ws://localhost:18789/`), token, and chat ID
5. Create any issue assigned to that agent
6. Trigger a run

**Expected:** Message relays to Telegram.
**Actual:** Run fails with schema validation error.

items.title: M2.3 Confirmation policy engine (3-tier + confirm_pending)
items.content:
## Goal
3-tier confirmation policy engine integrated into the per-call state machine between `Validated` and `Dispatched`.

## Context
- Scope: operator-layer
- Maps to: CONFIRMATION_POLICY.md, STATE_MACHINE.md, IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md §M2.3
- Affected modules: new `operator/ConfirmationPolicy.kt`, `openclaw/routing/IntentRouter.kt`, `gemini/SessionStateManager.kt`, `openclaw/ToolCallModels.kt` (add `confirm_pending` meta-tool)

## Current State
No confirmation logic. Current system prompt asks Gemini verbally to confirm outbound messages — brittle.

## Target State
`ConfirmationPolicy.evaluate(intent, sessionState) → Tier` where Tier is `AlwaysConfirm | ConditionalConfirm(prompt) | Implicit`. Tier 1/2 → `AwaitingConfirmation` state + `PendingConfirmation` on SessionStateManager.

`confirm_pending(pendingActionId, confirm)` meta-tool binds affirmatives to a specific ID. Bare "yes" never auto-confirms.

## Scope
- **In**: evaluator, phrasing, 30s timeout, supersede rules, `confirm_pending` meta-tool, entity-resolution validation layer.
- **Out**: phone overlay (M2.4).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Tier 1 action uses explicit-phrase confirmation
- [ ] Tier 2 action uses natural confirmation prompt
- [ ] Tier 3 action dispatches immediately
- [ ] Bare "yes" without ID match → Gemini told "no pending action to confirm"
- [ ] New confirmation-requiring proposal while one pending → old `Invalidated`
- [ ] 30s timeout expires with voice note
- [ ] Unit tests cover tier assignment for 10+ representative intents

## Dependencies
Requires M1.2, M1.3, M2.1.

## Rollback
Short-circuit `evaluate()` to always return `Implicit`. One line.
